free_text: Complete and Incomplete Fusion Reactions in 16O +
45Sc System: Measurement and Analysis of Excitation
Functions below 7 MeV/Nucleon
Jamal Mohammed College, Trichurapalli.
The targets of specpure 45Sc were prepared by vacuum
evaporation technique. These targets were deposited
on circular aluminium backings of uniform thickness
1.14mg/ cm2, spread over a circular area of
diameter 15 mm. Thickness of 45Sc was lying between
80 to 700 mu-g/cm2.
100 cm3 HPGe detector
The total absolute error in the measured
cross-section includes statistical error in the
measured peak areas together with systematic errors.
Fluctuations in the beam current have been
incorporated by taking the weighted average of beam
current.The error due to this factor is estimated to
be less than 4%.
The non-uniformity in the deposition of
target material introduces error less than 3% in the
estimation on number of nuclei present in the target.
Errors due to uncertainty in detector
efficiency were estimated to be less than 5%.
The dead time in the pulse processing
electronics may lead to a loss in the counts. By
adjusting sample-detector distance, dead-time
correction has been minimized, so that dead time is
limited below 10%, particularly for the cases where
the activity of irradiated sample is large. However,
the correction for it has been incorporated by
counting the sample in the live time mode of the
data acquisition system.
The errors due to decrease in the oxygen ion
beam intensity because of its scattering while
traversing through the stack, is estimated to be less
than 1%.
Errors associated with the straggling of the
ion-beam are estimated to be less than 2%
Data were received from Prof. M. Afzal Ansari
compiled by Vijay, Vidya, Jasminder & Megha.
